## Final Accepted Amendments

### `fr-7-5fb432a4`

finding_summary: [src/adapters/worktree.rs:161-172] `assume_unchanged_paths` misparses `git ls-files -z -s -v` output by splitting on every tab and taking `.nth(1)`. Git only uses the first tab as the metadata/path separator, so a legal assume-unchanged filename like `tab\tfile.txt` is truncated to `tab`. `remove_paths_from_index` then removes nothing, and the checkpoint tree still retains the assume-unchanged file. That means the new filtering logic is still incorrect for valid Git paths containing tabs. affected_files: src/adapters/worktree.rs, tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs. remediation_hint: split each record only once at the first tab (`splitn(2, ...)` / equivalent byte-slice logic) and add a regression next to `worktree_adapter_omits_assume_unchanged_paths_from_checkpoint_commits` for an assume-unchanged path containing a tab.
